                                        U.S. DEPARTMENT OF LABOR
                                                 Ray Marshall, Secretary


                                            BUREAU OF LABOR STATISTICS
                                              Julius Shiskin, Commissioner



                                        Employment and Earnings is prepared by the
                                    Division of Industry Employment Statistics and the
                                    Division of Employment and Unemployment Anal-
                                    ysis in collaboration with the Division of Special
                                    Publications. The data are collected by the Bureau
                                    of the Census (Department of Commerce), Employ-
                                    ment and Training Administration, State Employ-
                                    ment Security Agencies, and State Departments of
                                    Labor in cooperation with the Bureau of Labor
                                    Statistics. A brief description of the cooperative
                                    statistical programs of the BLS with these agencies
                                    is presented in the Explanatory Notes. The State
                                    agencies are listed on the inside back cover.
                                        Employment and Earnings may be ordered
                                    through the Superintendent of Documents, U.S.
                                    Government Printing Office, Washington, D.C.
                                    20402. Subscription price per year $24 domes-
                                    tic, and $30 foreign. Single copy $2.70. Prices
                                    are subject to change by the U.S. Government
                                    Printing Office.

                                         Communications on editorial matters should be
                                    addressed to: Editors, Employment and Earnings,
                                    Bureau of Labor Statistics,Washington, D.C. 20212.
                                    Inquiries regarding the text and Household Data
                                    should be addressed to: Attention of Gloria P,
                                    Green or Phone: (202) 523-1944. Inquiries relating
                                    to Establishment Data and all other tables should be
                                    addressed to: Attention of Chester L. Green or
                                     Phone: (202) 523-1759. Send correspondence on
                                     circulation and subscription matters (including ad-
                                     dress changes) to the Superintendent of Documents.

                                        The Secretary of Labor has determined that
                                    the publication of this periodical is necessary in
                                    the transaction of the public business required by
                                    law of this Department. Use of funds for printing
                                    this periodical has been approved by the Director
                                    of the Office of Management and Budget through
                                     February 28, 1981. Controlled circulation postage
                                    paid at Washington, D.C. and Baltimore, Md.

                                    Library of Congress Catalog Number 70-11379

                                        Unless identified as copyrighted, articles and
                                    tabulations in this publication are in the public
                                    domain and may be reproduced without permis-
                                    sion of the Federal Government. Please credit Em-
                                    ployment and Earnings, Bureau of Labor Statistics.

                         Employment and Unemployment Developments,
                                       December 1976
           Employment rose in December and unemployment de-            larger increase in the previous month, and, as in November,
       clined. The unemployment rate was 7.9 percent, down from         December's gain was concentrated among adult women.
       8.1 percent in November and similar to rates prevailing          Since the March 1975 recession low, total employment has

                                                                                  schedules are of the "shuttle" type, with space for each month
           COLLECTION
                                                                                  of the calendar year. The collection agency returns the schedule
               Payroll reports provide current information on wage and            to the respondent each month so that the next month's data
           salary employment, hours, earnings, and labor turnover in              can be entered. This procedure assures maximum comparability
           nonagriculturai establishments, by industry and geographic             and accuracy of reporting, since the respondent can see the
           location.                                                              figures he has reported for previous months.
                                                                                       Form BLS 790 provides for entry of data on the number of
           Federal-State cooperation                                              full- and part-time workers on the payrolls of nonagricultural
                                                                                  establishments and, for most industries, payroll and hours of
              Under cooperative arrangements with State agencies, the             production and related workers or nonsupervisory workers for
          respondent fills out a single employment or labor turnover              the pay period which includes the 12th of the month. Form DL
          reporting form, which is then used for national, State, and             1219 provides for the collection of information on the total
          area estimates. This eliminates duplicate reporting on the              number of accessions and separations, by type, during the
          part of respondents, and together with the use of identical             calendar month.
          techniques at the national and State levels, insures maximum
          comparability of estimates.
              State agencies mail the forms to the establishments and             CONCEPTS
          examine the returns for consistency, accuracy, and complete-
          ness. The States use the information to prepare State and area          Industrial classification
          series and then send the establishment data to the BLS for use in
          preparing the national series.                                             Establishments reporting on Form BLS 790 and Form DL
                                                                                 1219 are classified into industries on the basis of their principal
           Shuttle schedules                                                     product or activity determined from information on annual sales
                                                                                 volume. This information is collected each year on a supplement
              Two types of data collection schedules are used: Form BLS          to the monthly 790 or 1219 report. For an establishment

           individual establishments. This movement, which relates to a         month employment to that of the previous month is computed.
